title: stop integrity validate-workflow state corrupt
purpose: A4.4 defines stop integrity validate workflow state corrupt for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified.
scope: Owns `A4.4` only; siblings under `A4` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §3 — Branch A — Pursuit & control plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#3-branch-a-pursuit-control-plane).
behavior_steps: When preflight or post-step S0 runs `validate-workflow.py` against journal and state.json, any schema failure, conformance error, or dual-write mismatch triggers an A4.4 integrity stop before another [A2.2](A2.2-if-ready-execute-one-pipeline-step.md) phase is scheduled.

Pursuit halts immediately—the conductor does not spawn workers or advance next_action while harness memory is untrusted, so restart after manual edits or merge conflicts cannot duplicate or skip work on ambiguous state.

Recovery is deterministic-first: run repair scripts, restore a Plane H snapshot, or reconcile journal and state.json until validate-workflow passes and [A2.3](A2.3-post-step-route-tier-dual-write-increment.md) dual-write restores a single source of truth.

If automated repair cannot restore valid state, preflight escalates H2 with the validation log attached and goal.state stays blocked until an operator approves recovery—never continuing pursuit on corrupt memory.

On the next wake after recovery, [A2.1](A2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) re-runs integrity checks fail-closed so evidence paths and pursuit counters cannot attach to the wrong goal_id.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
goal_verify passes but H3 rejected â€” goal.state returns to pursuing with rejection notes.
Edge case `A4.4` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `A4.4`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Add `goal` block to state template and journal-keeper dual-write (v2.14).
Implement `scripts/goal-verify.py` stub aggregating task evidence paths.
Document `A4.4` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `A4.4`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: A4.4 defines stop integrity validate workflow state corrupt for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified. In practice, the system when preflight or post-step s0 runs `validate-workflow.py` against journal and state.json, any schema failure, conformance error, or dual-write mismatch triggers an a4.4 integrity stop before another [a2.2](a2.2-if-ready-execute-one-pipeline-step.md) phase is scheduled, then pursuit halts immediately—the conductor does not spawn workers or advance next_action while harness memory is untrusted, so restart after manual edits or merge conflicts cannot duplicate or skip work on ambiguous state.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-a 2026-06-28 -->

Integrity stops fire when the harness cannot trust its own state: `validate-workflow.py` fails, state.json is corrupt or schema-invalid, dual-write mismatch between journal and state, or artifact graph references missing nodes. Pursuit must not continue on ambiguous memory.

Recovery is deterministic-first: repair scripts, restore snapshot from Plane H, or escalate H2 with the validation log attached. Continuing pursuit on corrupt state duplicates work or attaches evidence to the wrong goal. This stop class is rare in production but critical for restart safety after manual edits or merge conflicts.
